title: Congenital Hydrocephalus: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Congenital Hydrocephalus
Identify key diagnostic features of Congenital Hydrocephalus
Apply clinical reasoning to Congenital Hydrocephalus management
Recognize common pitfalls in Congenital Hydrocephalus

sections.title: Overview of Congenital Hydrocephalus
sections.content:
**Congenital Hydrocephalus**

Aqueductal stenosis, communicating vs non-communicating, macrocephaly, VP shunt

**Key Points:**
• Most common cause: aqueductal stenosis (obstruction of cerebral aqueduct of Sylvius)
• Non-communicating (obstructive): blockage within ventricular system
• Communicating: impaired CSF absorption at arachnoid granulations

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Most common cause: aqueductal stenosis (obstruction of cerebral aqueduct of Sylvius)
• Non-communicating (obstructive): blockage within ventricular system

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Communicating: impaired CSF absorption at arachnoid granulations
• Signs: macrocephaly, bulging fontanelle, sunset eyes (upgaze palsy), irritability
• Treatment: VP shunt or endoscopic third ventriculostomy (ETV)

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Congenital Hydrocephalus:**

• Communicating: impaired CSF absorption at arachnoid granulations
• Signs: macrocephaly, bulging fontanelle, sunset eyes (upgaze palsy), irritability
• Treatment: VP shunt or endoscopic third ventriculostomy (ETV)

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Congenital Hydrocephalus?

options.text: Most common cause: aqueductal stenosis (obstruction of cerebral aqueduct of Sylvius)
options.explanation: Most common cause: aqueductal stenosis (obstruction of cerebral aqueduct of Sylvius)

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for Congenital Hydrocephalus?

options.text: Treatment: VP shunt or endoscopic third ventriculostomy (ETV)
options.explanation: Treatment: VP shunt or endoscopic third ventriculostomy (ETV)

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases
